vocab.meaning: tâm lý bầy đàn / xu hướng làm theo đám đông
vocab.example: I never really subscribed to a herd mentality; even as a toddler, I was quite content playing by myself.
Being extroverted by nature, I constantly sought out the company of my peers to engage in team sports.

vocab.meaning: sự biệt lập (thường dùng ẩn dụ cho việc thích ở một mình)
vocab.example: While some kids hated being alone, I actually relished my 'solitary confinement' in the attic where I could read in peace.

vocab.meaning: người năng nổ giao thiệp / người hướng ngoại
vocab.example: I was something of a social butterfly in primary school, always flitting from one group of friends to another.

vocab.meaning: tình bạn thân thiết, sự đồng cam cộng khổ
vocab.example: I preferred group activities because I thrived on the shared camaraderie that comes with being part of a team.
Due to my introverted tendencies, I found large groups quite draining and preferred the company of one or two close friends.

vocab.meaning: nỗ lực hợp tác / làm việc nhóm
vocab.example: Building giant Lego cities was a collaborative effort that taught me how to negotiate and share from a young age.

vocab.meaning: người thích làm việc độc lập / con sói đơn độc
vocab.example: In my early years, I was a bit of a lone wolf, preferring to solve puzzles and draw without any outside interference.

vocab.meaning: kỹ năng giao tiếp giữa người với người
vocab.example: Playing in a group was vital for me as it helped sharpen my interpersonal skills and emotional intelligence.

vocab.meaning: nơi trú ẩn cá nhân / không gian riêng tư
vocab.example: My bedroom was my personal sanctuary where I could indulge in solo hobbies like model airplane building.
